summary: Pelvic CT scans are required in renal cell carcinoma because they assess for local tumor spread including involvement of pelvic organs and vascular structures critical for accurate staging. Their presence directly impacts treatment planning and patient outcomes by informing decisions about surgical intervention versus systemic therapy.
